caption:
Stop wrapping everything in new Promise().

Promise.withResolvers() gives you resolve and reject directly. No callback nesting.

ES2024. All browsers. Zero polyfills.

Which pattern do you use? A or B?

#javascript #promises #es2024 #webdev #coding #programming #developer #async #codingtips #cleancode

slides.title: Which Promise pattern is cleaner?
slides.problem: Create a promise and resolve it externally

a.label: new Promise()

slides.title: B wins. No wrapper needed.

verdicts.title: Leaking resolve/reject outside
verdicts.text: You create variables, then assign inside the callback. It's a hack everyone uses because there was no alternative.

verdicts.title: Clean destructuring
verdicts.text: One line. Get promise, resolve, and reject as separate values. No callbacks. No variable hoisting.

slides.takeaway: Promise.withResolvers() kills the most common Promise anti-pattern.

slides.title: Where it actually shines.

items.text: No more new Promise() wrapper hack
Works in Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge
